default_annotator.report_text:
The shim injected a malicious in-memory patch into the Services Control Manager ("services.exe") process, and then spawned a CARBANAK backdoor process. FIN7 leveraged an application shim database to achieve persistent access on systems in multiple environments.

FIN7 used a custom Base64 encoded PowerShell script, which ran the "sdbinst.exe" utility to register a custom shim database #le containing a patch onto a system. PowerShell is used to run a utility (sdbinst.exe) to register a shim database file, which is part of the persistence mechanism.

obtained an additional shellcode payload stored in a registry key. the shellcode is stored in a registry key for later retrieval and execution.

The second stage shellcode launched the CARBANAK DLL (stored in a registry key), which spawned an instance of Service Host ("svchost.exe") and injected itself into that process The CARBANAK DLL is injected into the svchost.exe process, demonstrating the use of process injection techniques.

The new "ScRegisterTCPEndpoint" function (shellcode) contained a reference to the path of "\REGISTRY\MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microso$\ DRM", which is a registry location where additional malicious shellcode and the CARBANAK DLL payload was stored on the system the ScRegisterTCPEndpoint function references a registry path where malicious shellcode and the CARBANAK DLL are stored.

The shellcode stored within the registry path "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microso$\DRM" used the API function "RtlDecompressBu%er" to decompress the payload The use of RtlDecompressBuffer to handle decompression of the payload indicates leveraging native APIs to perform specific tasks.

Once loaded in memory, it created a new process named "svchost.exe" that contained the CARBANAK DLL. Adversaries may create or modify system-level processes to repeatedly execute malicious payloads as part of persistence.
